title: selaginella grandis
intro: Selaginella grandis, also known as the resurrection plant, is a species of evergreen fern-like plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to help treat various health issues such as respiratory problems, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Selaginella grandis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antioxidant, while also exhibiting immunomodulatory and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Selaginella grandis are typically made from the leaves and stems of the plant, and can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures.
